title: MorseEEG-ATP
description: MorseEEG-ATP is a multitask electroencephalography dataset comprising synchronously recorded EEG and behavioral data from 32 healthy adult participants designed to investigate auditory temporal processing and temporal pattern discrimination using Morse code stimuli. The dataset includes raw 62-channel EEG recordings at 600 Hz, minimally preprocessed derivatives, trial-aligned event annotations, and behavioral measures across two discrimination tasks (dot-count and first-last match) presented at three transmission speeds (15, 30, and 40 characters per minute). This resource bridges the gap between low-level acoustic encoding and higher-level cognitive processing by providing a controlled, parameterizable paradigm for studying the extraction and discrimination of temporal patterns governed by explicit temporal rules.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a g.tec medical engineering g.HIamp system with 62 scalp electrodes arranged according to the International 10-20 system, sampled at 600 Hz with online bandpass filtering (0.1-100 Hz) and notch filtering (48-52 Hz). The reference electrode was placed at the right earlobe. Stimuli were presented and behavioral responses collected using E-Prime 3.0. Each trial consisted of a fixation period, stimulus presentation, response window, and jittered inter-trial interval.
